q: The study of interference and diffraction, for which the ray approximation of geometrical optics is not valid, are dealt with ___.
In wave optics, Huygens' principle explained how the light propagates from a ___.
Huygens' principle states that every point on a wavefront may be considered as a source of ___.
The new position of wavefront is the ___ of the wavelets.
A wavefront is defined as a surface associated with a propagating wave which is a locus of all points in the wave that have the ___.
Wavefront is always perpendicular to the direction of propagation (or) ___.
The spherical wavefront from a source transforms into a plane wavefront when it is ___ from the source.
The phase angle is one of the important characteristics of the ___.
If phase difference is $0, 2\pi, 4\pi, 6\pi, ...$, these two positions are in ___.
If phase difference are $\pi, 3\pi, 5\pi, ...$, these two positions are completely ___.
Path difference indicates the distance between two points measured along the ___ of the wave through the medium.
If path difference is $0, \lambda, 2\lambda, 3\lambda, ...$, it is said in ___.
If path difference are $\frac{\lambda}{2}, \frac{3\lambda}{2}, \frac{5\lambda}{2}, ...$, it is said completely ___.

The relation between phase difference and path difference is expressed as $\Delta \phi = \frac{2\pi}{\lambda} \Delta x$. Here $\Delta \phi$ is phase difference between two waves of the same wavelength $\lambda$ and $\Delta x$ is the ___ of these waves.
Wave interference is the phenomenon that occurs when two waves meet while traveling along the same ___.
Interference of light waves is defined as the modification in the distribution of energy (intensity) when two or more waves ___ each other.
To observe interference of light waves, the two sources of light must be ___ with respect to each other.
If the waves emitted from two sources have the same frequency and constant phase difference, the sources are ___ sources.
Thomas Young, English physician and physicist, obtained convincing evidence for the ___ nature of light.
Monochromatic light (a particular wavelength) from a ___ source are used in his experiment.
When a plane wave passes through a slit, a ___ emerges from a slit.
The interference pattern consists of a series of bright and dark parallel lines called ___.
The bright fringe at centre is called ___.
If the path difference is equal to an integral number of wavelengths, the waves will interfere ___.
Constructive interference leads to a ___ on the screen.
If the path difference is equal to a half-integral number of wavelengths, the waves will interfere ___.
Destructive interference leads to a ___ on the screen.
The zeroth order ($m = 0$) bright corresponds to the central bright line at $\theta = 0$, and the first-order brights ($m = 1$) can be seen on either side of the ___.
The distance of $m^{th}$ bright fringe from the central bright line is $y_{m} = m \frac{\lambda D}{d}$, where $m$ is called the ___.
For $D \gg d$, $\theta \approx \theta'$, where $\theta$ is the angle from original direction of ___.

Under the condition that $\theta$ is small, the approximation can be used $\theta (\text{rad}) \approx \sin \theta \approx \tan \theta \approx \frac{y}{D}$. Therefore, $d \sin \theta = m \lambda$ becomes $d \frac{y}{D} = m \lambda$, or $y = m \frac{\lambda D}{d}$. Here $y$ is distance of the $m^{th}$ bright fringe from the ___.
Distance between two adjacent bright (or dark) fringes is called the ___.
The position of fringes and fringe width depend on the ___ of light source.
A wavefront is a locus of all points in the wave that have the ___.
The angular position of a point of a periodic wave is known as the ___.
Secondary wavelets spread out in all directions with the speed ___.
Young was able to explain this result as ___ phenomenon.
When this circular wave passes through two slits, each slit acts as a secondary source of ___.
At any time, two waves emitted from each slit are of same frequency, same amplitude and are in ___.
If the waves emitted from two sources have the same frequency and constant phase difference, the sources are coherent sources. This means that the light sources must maintain a ___.
For dark fringes, $y = (m + \frac{1}{2}) \frac{\lambda D}{d}$ where $y$ is distance of the $m^{th}$ dark fringe from the ___.
For bright fringe to be formed at P, the path difference is equal to an integral number of ___.
The emerging light arrives at the second screen which has two parallel narrow slits $S_{1}$ and $S_{2}$ separated by a distance ___.
A screen is placed at a large distance $D$ away from the ___.

The interference pattern consists of a series of bright and dark parallel lines called fringes. The formation of interference can be explained as follows. The waves spread out in all directions after passing through ___.

The distance of $m^{th}$ order bright fringe is $y_{m} = m \frac{\lambda D}{d}$. The distance of $(m+1)^{th}$ order bright fringe is $y_{(m+1)} = (m+1) \frac{\lambda D}{d}$. Fringe width is $\Delta y = (m+1) \frac{\lambda D}{d} - m \frac{\lambda D}{d} = \frac{\lambda D}{d}$. Thus, the fringe width $\Delta y = \frac{\lambda D}{d}$ is ___.
In Figure 8.2, phase angles of point A and point B are 0 and $\frac{\pi}{2}$ respectively. This refers to the phase angle for a point on a ___.
Wave optics includes the wave characteristics of ___.
Huygens' principle states that every point on a wavefront may be considered as a source of secondary wavelets. The new position of wavefront is the ___ of the wavelets.

The study of interference and diffraction, for which the ray approximation of geometrical optics is not valid, are dealt with wave optics (physical optics). Wave optics includes the wave characteristics of light. In wave optics, Huygens' principle explained how the light propagates from a ___.
The position of fringes and fringe width depend on the ___ of light source.
Bright and dark fringes are formed alternatively where bright fringe has ___ intensity.
In Young's double slit experiment, the intensity of dark fringe is ___.
The distance between the first and second order bright fringes is equal to the ___ width.
Diffraction is defined as the ___ of waves around the corners of an obstacle.
The amount of bending depends on the wavelength of light relative to the ___ of the aperture.
If the aperture is much larger than the wavelength of light, the bending will be almost ___.
A very simple demonstration of light diffraction can be conducted by placing the sharp edges of a ___ blade in front of intense blue light.
There are two main classes of diffraction, which are known as Fresnel diffraction and ___ diffraction.
Fresnel diffraction: The source of light and the screen are at a ___ distance from the obstacle causing the diffraction.
Fraunhofer diffraction: There is an ___ distance between the source and the screen from the obstacle.
When light passes through a single slit whose width $a$ is of the order of the wavelength of the light, then the diffraction pattern is focussed on a screen that is a distance ___ away from the slit.
The diffraction pattern consists of a bright central portion called the ___.
Each successive bright fringes becomes less ___ proceeding outward, away from the central maximum.
These bright and dark fringes are created by ___ of diffracted light waves.
The angular position $\theta$ of any point on the screen is measured from ___ of the slit.
The path difference between two waves from the centre and top (or) the centre and bottom edge of the slit is ___ and these two waves are exactly out of phase.
When they arrive at the screen, they interfere destructively and produces a dark fringe (minimum intensity) called the ___.
The first minima are seen on the either side ___ bright maximum.
To get the first minimum (dark) the path difference between two waves from the centre and top (or) the centre and bottom edge of the slit is ___.
For the second minimum, the slit is divided into ___ parts.
Generally for the $m^{\text{th}}$ minimum, ___ where $m = 1, 2, 3, \dots$.
The position of $m^{\text{th}}$ minimum $y$ is the distance from the ___ maximum.
The width of the central maximum $= 2 \times$ position of the ___ minimum.
The width of the central maximum depends on width of the ___.
The ___ slit causes the wider central maximum.
Most modern-day applications of slit interference use not just two slits but many, approaching several thousand per ___ for practical purposes.
This optical instrument is called the ___ , an important tool in optical analysis.
A diffraction grating is an optical device which consists of a large number of parallel slits of same width separated by equal ___ spaces.
A diffraction grating can disperse light into its ___ components.
Therefore, it is used for the purpose of resolving light into ___.
Diffraction grating is thus widely used as dispersive elements in ___ instruments.
Two types of grating are ___ grating and reflection grating.
The number of lines (or slits) per unit length (lines per cm) is called the ___ constant.
The condition for maximum intensity is the same as that for a Young's ___.
For maximum intensity to be formed at P, the path difference of two waves from adjacent slits is equal to an ___ number of wavelengths.
If the number of slits per unit length is $N$, $d$ is equal to ___.
There is no ___ of incident white light at the centre (i.e, $m = 0$).
In the first order diffraction spectrum, longer wavelength (red light) diffracts at a greater angle than shorter wavelength (___ light).
A compact disc is an example of ___ gratings.
